_publications._publicationTitle: Nf1 heterozygous mice recapitulate the anthropometric and metabolic features of human neurofibromatosis type 1.
_publications._context: Nf1 heterozygous (Nf1+/-) mice accurately reflect the dominant inheritance of NF1 and are regularly employed as a model of NF1 | Nf1 +/− mice were generated as previously described (11). In brief, Nf1 +/− mice maintained in the 129/Sv background (12) were crossed with wild-type C57Bl/6 mice. | male and female Nf1 (+/-) mice were mated for 12–16 hours | In order to obtain Nf1 (-/-) embryos, male and female Nf1 (+/-) mice were mated | Mice heterozygous for the Nf1 mutation on a background of C57BL/6J were originally developed by Dr. Tyler Jacks | Nf1 heterozygous (Nf1+/–, C57BL/6) mice have been previously described | heterozygous germline knockout mouse model of Neurofibromatosis type 1 (Nf1 ± mice) | R26tdTom/tdTom, Nf1flox/flox; Nf1flox/flox; Nf1+/−; and B6D2F1 | Nf1+/- and wildtype mice were generated as previously described. In brief, Nf1+/- mice maintained in the 129/Sv background were crossed with wild-type C57Bl/6 mice. | Generation of the...Nf1 (+/- Jacks et al., 1994)...In MNTB principal neurons of Nf1 (+/-) mice the average delay between EPSPs and APs was slightly smaller | Nf1 +/− mice were obtained from Dr. Tyler Jacks at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology | Optic chiasm was microdissected from 3-month-old Nf1 flox/flox, Nf1 +/− and Nf1 +/− GFAP CKO mice | Nf1+/- mice were obtained from Tyler Jacks (Massachusetts Institute of Technology, Cambridge, MA, USA) and backcrossed 13 generations into the C57BL/6J strain. | Several Nf1 alleles were used in these studies including Nf1 (germline mutant) mice (− Jacks et al., 1994) | Nf1+/ − mice [ 30 ] were bred with Nf1 f/wt mice to produce Nf1 flox/mut mice | Nf1 +/− mice were obtained from Dr Tyler Jacks at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (Cambridge, MA, USA) in a C57BL/6J.129 background and backcrossed for 13 generations into a C57BL/6J strain | statin-mediated inhibition of 3-hydroxy-3-methylglutaryl coenzyme A reductase restores the cognitive deficits in an NF1 mouse model
